title: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled

seo.imageAlt: Platter of vibrant red Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled, garnished with cilantro and lemon wedges.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the smoky, authentic flavor of Tandoori Chicken, perfectly grilled over charcoal for an unforgettable Indian dinner.

description: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled offers an authentic taste of India with its smoky, succulent chicken marinated in a vibrant blend of yogurt and spices. This classic dish is renowned for its tender texture and irresistible char, achieved by grilling over hot coals. Perfect for a hearty dinner, it brings the flavors of a traditional tandoor to your backyard grill.

ingredients.item: Whole Chicken or Chicken Pieces (thighs, drumsticks, breasts)

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken
instructions.text: Wash the chicken thoroughly and pat it completely dry with paper towels. Using a sharp knife, make deep gashes or slits all over the chicken pieces, ensuring they penetrate the flesh but do not cut through the bone. This allows the marinade to absorb deeply.

instructions.title: First Marinade Application
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken with 1 tablespoon of lemon juice and 1 teaspoon of salt. Rub this mixture well into the chicken, making sure it gets into all the slits. Let it rest for 10 minutes. This step helps tenderize the chicken and adds initial flavor.

instructions.title: Prepare the Second Marinade
instructions.text: In a separate bowl, whisk together the plain yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, remaining lemon juice, Kashmiri red chili powder, regular red chili powder, turmeric powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, garam masala, remaining salt, black pepper powder, and mustard oil. If using, add the red food color for a classic vibrant hue. Mix until smooth and well combined.

instructions.title: Second Marinade and Marination
instructions.text: Pour the prepared yogurt-spice marinade over the chicken in the bowl. Using your hands, thoroughly coat each piece of chicken, ensuring the marinade penetrates deeply into the slits. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or preferably overnight, for best flavor and tenderness.

instructions.title: Prepare the Charcoal Grill
instructions.text: About 30-40 minutes before grilling, prepare your charcoal grill. Arrange the charcoal to create a two-zone fire: one side with direct high heat and the other with indirect medium heat. Ensure the coals are glowing hot and covered with a light layer of ash.

instructions.title: Grill the Tandoori Chicken
instructions.text: Lightly oil the grill grates to prevent sticking. Place the marinated chicken pieces on the direct heat side of the grill. Cook for 5-7 minutes per side, turning occasionally, until they get a good char. Then, move the chicken to the indirect heat side, close the lid, and continue cooking for 20-25 minutes, or until the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C). Baste the chicken with ghee or melted butter every 10 minutes during this stage to keep it moist and flavorful.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Once cooked through, remove the Tandoori Chicken from the grill. Garnish with fresh chopped coriander leaves and serve immediately with lemon wedges, mint chutney, sliced onions, or naan bread. Enjoy the smoky, tender, and incredibly flavorful Tandoori Chicken!

tips: For the most tender and flavorful Tandoori Chicken, marinate the chicken for at least 12-24 hours. The longer it marinates, the deeper the flavors will be.
Maintain a consistent medium-high heat on your charcoal grill for even cooking and a beautiful char without burning. Use a meat thermometer to ensure the chicken is cooked to 165°F (74°C).
Basting with ghee or melted butter during the last stage of grilling not only adds a rich flavor but also helps keep the chicken moist and gives it a glossy finish.
article:
Embark on a culinary journey to the heart of India with one of its most beloved and iconic dishes: Tandoori Chicken. While traditionally cooked in a scorching hot clay oven known as a tandoor, the magic of Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled brings that authentic, smoky flavor right to your backyard. This Indian dinner recipe is not just a meal; it's an experience, transforming humble chicken into a succulent, spice-infused masterpiece with tantalizing charred edges and an incredibly juicy interior. Perfect for gatherings or a special family dinner, mastering this dish will make you the star of any barbecue.

## What is Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled?

Tandoori Chicken is a classic Indian dish featuring chicken marinated in yogurt and a vibrant blend of aromatic spices, then traditionally cooked at high heat in a tandoor. The "Charcoal Grilled" aspect refers to cooking this marinated chicken over hot charcoal, mimicking the intense heat and infusing it with an irresistible smoky flavor that's hard to achieve with other cooking methods. The marinade, typically a mix of full-fat yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, red chili powder, turmeric, cumin, coriander, garam masala, and lemon juice, acts as both a tenderizer and a flavor powerhouse. The yogurt's lactic acid breaks down the chicken fibers, resulting in incredibly tender meat, while the spices penetrate deep, creating a complex and unforgettable taste. When cooked over charcoal, the chicken develops a beautiful crust, with slightly charred bits that add texture and depth, contrasting wonderfully with the moist and flavorful interior. This method brings out the best in the chicken, making Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled a truly exceptional dish.

## Ingredients for Tandoori Chicken

To create the perfect Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled, quality ingredients are key. Here’s what you’ll need:

*   **For the Chicken:**
    *   2-3 lbs bone-in, skinless chicken pieces (legs, thighs, or a whole chicken cut into 8 pieces). Scoring the chicken deeply helps the marinade penetrate.
*   **For the First Marinade:**
    *   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
    *   1 teaspoon salt
    *   1/2 teaspoon red chili powder (or Kashmiri chili powder for color)
    *   1 tablespoon lemon juice
*   **For the Second Marinade (Yogurt Marinade):**
    *   1 cup full-fat plain yogurt (Greek yogurt can be used, but thin it slightly with water)
    *   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
    *   1 teaspoon red chili powder (adjust to your spice preference)
    *   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
    *   1 teaspoon cumin powder
    *   1 teaspoon coriander powder
    *   1 teaspoon garam masala
    *   1/2 teaspoon black pepper
    *   1/2 teaspoon salt (or to taste)
    *   1 tablespoon mustard oil (or any neutral oil like vegetable/canola oil)
    *   1-2 drops red or orange food coloring (optional, for authentic color)
    *   Juice of 1/2 lemon

## How to Make Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled

Creating this incredible Indian dinner recipe is a multi-step process, but each step is crucial for the ultimate flavor and texture:

1.  **Prepare the Chicken:** Wash the chicken pieces thoroughly and pat them completely dry with paper towels. Using a sharp knife, make deep gashes (2-3 per piece) into the thickest parts of the chicken. This ensures the marinade penetrates deeply and the chicken cooks evenly.

2.  **First Marinade:** In a large bowl, combine the chicken with the ingredients for the first marinade: ginger-garlic paste, salt, red chili powder, and lemon juice. Rub this mixture all over the chicken, ensuring it gets into the scores. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or up to an hour. This initial marinade helps tenderize the chicken and build a foundational flavor.

3.  **Second Marinade (Yogurt Marinade):** While the chicken is in its first marinade, prepare the second. In another bowl, whisk together the full-fat yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, red chili powder, turmeric, cumin, coriander, garam masala, black pepper, salt, mustard oil, food coloring (if using), and lemon juice until well combined.

4.  **Combine Marinades:** Add the first-marinated chicken to the yogurt mixture. Ensure every piece is thoroughly coated. Cover the bowl tightly with plastic wrap and refrigerate for a minimum of 6 hours, or ideally overnight (12-24 hours). The longer the marination, the more flavorful and tender your Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled will be.

5.  **Prepare the Grill:** About 30-45 minutes before you plan to cook, prepare your charcoal grill. Arrange the charcoal to create a two-zone fire: one side with direct high heat and the other with indirect medium heat. Ensure the coals are glowing red with a light ash coating. Clean the grill grates and oil them lightly to prevent sticking.

6.  **Grill the Chicken:** Remove the chicken from the fridge about 30 minutes before grilling to bring it closer to room temperature. Scrape off any excess marinade to prevent burning, but ensure a good coating remains. Place the chicken pieces directly over the high-heat charcoal. Sear for 2-3 minutes per side until you get a good char and color.

7.  **Cook Through:** Once charred, move the chicken to the indirect heat side of the grill. Close the lid and continue to cook, turning occasionally, for 20-30 minutes, or until the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C) and the juices run clear. During the last 10 minutes of cooking, you can baste the chicken with a little melted butter or ghee for extra richness and shine.

8.  **Rest and Serve:** Once cooked, remove the Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled from the grill and let it rest for 5-10 minutes before serving. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring moist and tender results.

## Tips for the Best Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken

*   **Full-Fat Yogurt is Key:** It’s thicker, clings better to the chicken, and its fat content helps keep the chicken moist and prevents the marinade from curdling on the grill.
*   **Don’t Skip Scoring:** Deep cuts allow the marinade to penetrate the meat, infusing it with flavor and ensuring even cooking.
*   **Marinate Longer:** The longer the chicken marinates, the more tender and flavorful it becomes. Overnight (12-24 hours) is ideal.
*   **Pat Dry Before Marinating:** Excess moisture on the chicken skin can prevent proper browning and charring.
*   **Regulate Grill Temperature:** Start with high heat for charring, then move to indirect heat to cook through without burning the exterior.
*   **Don't Overcrowd the Grill:** Cook in batches if necessary to ensure even heat distribution and proper charring. Overcrowding lowers the grill temperature.
*   **Rest the Chicken:** Always rest grilled chicken for a few minutes after cooking. This allows the juices to settle back into the meat, ensuring a more tender and juicy result.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings

Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled is a star on its own, but it truly shines when served with complementary Indian sides.

*   **Breads:** Warm naan bread, roti, or paratha are perfect for scooping up the flavorful chicken and any accompanying sauces.
*   **Chutneys & Dips:** A cooling mint-coriander chutney or a simple raita (yogurt dip with cucumber and spices) provides a refreshing contrast to the rich, spicy chicken.
*   **Salads:** A fresh kachumber salad (diced cucumber, tomato, and onion with a lemon dressing) or simple sliced red onions and lemon wedges offer a tangy crunch.
*   **Rice:** A side of plain basmati rice or fragrant jeera rice (cumin rice) can round out the meal beautifully.
*   **Beverages:** A sweet or savory lassi (yogurt drink) or a chilled Indian beer pairs wonderfully with the robust flavors.

## Variations & FAQs

**Variations:**
*   **Paneer/Fish/Prawn Tandoori:** The same marinade can be used for paneer (Indian cheese), firm white fish, or large prawns. Adjust cooking times accordingly.
*   **Spicy vs. Mild:** Easily adjust the amount of red chili powder to suit your heat preference. Kashmiri chili powder adds color with less heat.
*   **Oven/Gas Grill Method:** While charcoal is best, you can use a gas grill (adding wood chips for smoke) or even bake/broil it in an oven. For oven-cooked, a final broil helps achieve some char.

**FAQs:**

*   **Can I use skin-on chicken?** While traditional Tandoori Chicken is skinless, you can leave the skin on for extra crispiness and flavor, though it will absorb less marinade directly.
*   **Why is my chicken dry?** Most likely, it was overcooked. Use a meat thermometer to ensure it reaches 165°F (74°C) and no more. Insufficient marination or not enough fat in the marinade can also contribute.
*   **Can I prepare the marinade ahead of time?** Absolutely! The marinade can be made a day or two in advance and stored in the refrigerator.
*   **What if I don't have a charcoal grill?** A gas grill can be used, and you can add a smoky flavor using wood chips in a smoker box. For an authentic smoky touch indoors, perform a "dhungar" by placing a small bowl with a hot piece of charcoal and a teaspoon of ghee/oil into the cooked chicken, covering it immediately for a few minutes.

Bringing the authentic flavors of an Indian tandoor to your home grill is a rewarding experience. This Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled recipe promises a vibrant, smoky, and utterly delicious Indian dinner that will impress everyone at your table. So fire up your grill, embrace the spices, and enjoy this magnificent culinary creation!
